Patch Set 2:

I remember this was added to catch cases where obd was null. Could you at least list the cases where this is happening, ideally the backtrace.
If you just drop those debugging, this is just cancelling the effort to track obd == NULL. 

The Gerrit comments of the related patchset (from LU-15256) as the explanation.

Patch Set 2:

> If you just drop those debugging, this is just cancelling the effort to track obd == NULL. 
for majority of developers those messages just consume logspace adding 0 value, IMHO.
say, I do have a number of small patches adding specific debug to catch specific problems, but if we all start to add those to master, then.. not sure this is a good idea.
it's easy to track as a separate tiny patch to run/collect logs in AT when needed.

It seems like in some places (lustre/ptlrpc/service.c) obd_get_at_max() is intentionally called with NULL. That's probably defeating the purpose of this logging. If we keep this logging, then that caller should be fixed.

parents.subject: LU-17000 utils: In mydaemon() check after calling open()

commit.subject: LU-17285 obdclass: remove debug from obd_get_at_*
commit.message:
LU-17285 obdclass: remove debug from obd_get_at_*

Removing debugging CDEBUG() from obd_get_at_*() helpers.
Messages like the following:
00010000:00100000:0.0:1699657756.854481:0:27978:0:
	(ldlm_request.c:181:ldlm_cp_timeout()) NULL obd

Fixes: 0f2bc318d7 ("LU-15246 ptlrpc: per-device adaptive timeout parameters")
Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: Iaaa964d4e3ca62de5fb273f865d7cd4c3a4f9a29
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/53106
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
